rag_rules: Place AGREE contracts in component type scopes for port assumptions/guarantees, and in implementation scopes for subcomponent bindings.
Use annex agree {** ... **}; blocks for AGREE content, with type scopes for constants and equations, implementation scopes for assignments.
generic_rules: Ensure AGREE expressions use only visible identifiers and preserve type consistency.
Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations and nested if-then-else for branching logic.
Write guarantees relating visible outputs to visible inputs in component type contracts.
Do not reference implementation-only subcomponents in component type annexes.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE syntax uses assume/guarantee, eq, const, and assign with proper type declarations.
Generated AGREE code must not introduce package-qualified identifiers not present in the model.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-level bindings in component type scopes.
Do not reference AADL properties as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed as typed ports or constants.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property names as AGREE variables
AGREE annex placement in requirement analysis
